items.text: norn-macros crate exists as a workspace member with proc-macro = true in Cargo.toml
norn-macros depends only on proc-macro2, quote, and syn (full + extra-traits features)
norn-macros Cargo.toml declares unsafe_code = deny and pedantic clippy lints matching workspace standard
norn crate declares norn-macros as a dependency
norn crate re-exports ToolArgs derive macro via pub use norn_macros::ToolArgs in tool module
norn crate re-exports tool_follow_ups macro via pub use norn_macros::tool_follow_ups
ToolArgs derive generates a json_schema() -> serde_json::Value inherent method on the annotated struct
Generated schema has type: object with properties matching struct field names
Generated schema has additionalProperties: false by default
Fields without #[serde(default)] and not Option<T> appear in the required array
Fields with #[serde(default)] are omitted from the required array
Fields with #[serde(default = "fn")] are omitted from the required array
Doc comments on struct fields become description values on the corresponding schema properties
Multi-line doc comments are joined with spaces into a single description string
Property order in generated schema matches field declaration order
String and &str fields generate {"type": "string"}
bool fields generate {"type": "boolean"}
Signed integer fields (i8 through i64, isize) generate {"type": "integer"}
Unsigned integer fields (u8 through u64, usize) generate {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0}
Float fields (f32, f64) generate {"type": "number"}
Option<T> fields generate the inner type's schema and omit the field from required
Vec<T> fields generate {"type": "array", "items": <schema of T>}
HashMap<String, T> fields generate {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": <schema of T>}
serde_json::Value fields generate {} (any type accepted)
Nested structs with #[derive(ToolArgs)] generate inline object schemas with their own properties
Unsupported types produce compile_error! with a message naming the type and suggesting #[tool_args(schema = {...})]
Unit-variant-only enums generate {"type": "string", "enum": [...]} with variant names
Enum variant doc comments are collected into the enum-level description
#[serde(rename_all)] on enums transforms variant names in the enum array
#[serde(rename)] on individual variants overrides the variant name in the enum array
Tagged enums (#[serde(tag = "...")]) generate oneOf with discriminator const on each variant
Untagged enums (#[serde(untagged)]) generate oneOf without discriminator
Adjacently tagged enums (#[serde(tag = "t", content = "c")]) generate the correct two-field schema per variant
#[serde(rename = "x")] on a field changes the property name in the schema to x
#[serde(rename_all = "...")] on the struct transforms all property names
#[serde(skip)] omits the field from the schema entirely
#[serde(skip_deserializing)] omits the field from the schema entirely
#[serde(skip_serializing)] keeps the field in the schema (it still deserializes)
#[serde(flatten)] merges the nested struct's properties into the parent schema
#[serde(flatten)] on a non-struct type produces compile_error!
#[tool_args(schema = {...})] replaces the generated schema for that field with the literal JSON
#[tool_args(description = "...")] overrides the doc-comment description
#[tool_args(skip)] excludes the field from the schema without affecting deserialization
#[tool_args(required)] forces inclusion in required even with #[serde(default)]
#[tool_args(additional_properties)] sets additionalProperties: true on the field's object schema
Fields without doc comments and without #[tool_args(skip)] or #[serde(skip)] produce compile_error!
Compile errors from the macro point to the offending field via syn::Error::new_spanned
ToolArgs derive does not interfere with #[derive(Deserialize)] applied to the same struct
tool_follow_ups! macro generates code that constructs Vec<FollowUpAction> from declarative action definitions
Each action declaration supports name, description, when (condition closure), expires, and overrides fields
Description field supports interpolation from output fields via format-string syntax
Expiry shorthand (FileModified(path), AnyFileModified, TurnScoped, Never) expands to ExpiryCondition construction
when condition is evaluated against ToolOutput at runtime to filter applicable follow-ups
Overrides are expressed as JSON literal that gets stored on the FollowUpAction
Existing tools can use #[derive(ToolArgs)] without changing their Tool trait implementation beyond input_schema()
Hand-written input_schema() methods coexist with ToolArgs-derived json_schema() during incremental migration
Generated schemas for EditArgs, BashArgs, and PatchArgs are byte-identical to current hand-written schemas
